title: Add render startup health gate
summary: Create a formal health gate module that aggregates shader compilation checks and geometry attribute validation into a single pass/fail checkpoint, invoked before the render loop enters steady-state. Failures block steady-state entry and emit structured console diagnostics.

steps.description: New module that orchestrates startup health checks. Exports a `runStartupHealthGate(renderer, scene, camera, geometrySystems)` function that: (1) calls `renderer.compile(scene, camera)` and checks `ShaderErrorCollector` for errors, (2) iterates all geometry systems and runs `validateGeometryAttributes()` on each mesh's BufferGeometry against REQUIRED_ATTRIBUTES, (3) returns a `HealthGateResult` object with `{ passed: boolean, shaderErrors: ShaderError[], geometryErrors: GeometryValidationError[] }`, (4) logs structured failure reasons to console.error with a '[EAVI health-gate]' prefix when any check fails.

Replace the existing inline `validateShaderCompilation()` call in the geometry initialization block of `startLoop()` with a call to `runStartupHealthGate()`. If the gate fails, set a `healthGateFailed` flag that prevents the loop from transitioning to steady-state rendering (keeps showing placeholder, logs failure). The render loop should NOT throw — it stays alive showing the placeholder so the developer can read the console.

Add `HealthGateResult` interface to src/visual/types.ts so it can be referenced by both healthGate.ts and renderLoop.ts. Fields: `passed: boolean`, `shaderErrors: Array<{ shaderType: string; message: string }>`, `geometryErrors: Array<{ attribute: string; reason: string; systemName?: string }>`.

Create tests/visual/healthGate.test.ts covering: (1) gate passes when shaders compile and geometry is valid, (2) gate fails and logs when shader compilation has errors, (3) gate fails and logs when geometry attributes are missing or have wrong itemSize or non-finite values, (4) gate fails and logs when both shader and geometry errors exist, (5) returned result object contains structured error details. Mock WebGLRenderer, ShaderErrorCollector, and BufferGeometry as done in existing test files.

steps.action: Write integration test for render loop health gate blocking
steps.description: Add test cases to tests/visual/scene-integration.test.ts (or a new tests/visual/healthGate-integration.test.ts) verifying: (1) render loop enters steady-state when health gate passes, (2) render loop stays on placeholder and does not enter steady-state when health gate fails, (3) console.error is called with structured failure info on gate failure.

Run `npm run build` to confirm no type errors or build failures. Run `npm test` to confirm all tests pass. Manual check: load dev server, confirm no console errors and scene renders normally (health gate passes in happy path).

testStrategy: Unit tests mock the WebGL renderer and geometry to simulate both passing and failing scenarios. Integration tests verify the render loop respects the gate result. Existing tests for shaderValidation and geometryValidator remain unchanged — healthGate composes them rather than replacing them.

acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Startup verifies shader compilation stat: Step 1 (healthGate.ts calls renderer.compile + checks ShaderErrorCollector) and Step 2 (renderLoop invokes gate before steady-state)
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Startup verifies required geometry attri: Step 1 (healthGate.ts validates all geometry systems against REQUIRED_ATTRIBUTES)
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Startup logs a clear failure reason for: Step 1 (structured console.error with [EAVI health-gate] prefix, listing each error)
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Stories touching rendering are not compl: Step 2 (render loop stays on placeholder, never transitions to steady-state) — this is enforced by the gate blocking the transition

risks: The existing inline validateShaderCompilation() in renderLoop.ts throws on failure — the new gate must replace this with non-throwing behavior that blocks steady-state instead, to keep the placeholder visible for debugging.
ModeManager.initAllForValidation() initializes all three geometry systems — the health gate must validate all three, not just the active one.
